mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases (including FLT3/VEGFR/PDGFR) to suppress proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Approved for RCC/GIST; notes activity against FLT3/VEGFR/PDGFR; potency band ~0.1–5 µM

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family kinases and ABL, potentially impairing signaling dependencies in AML subsets
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; noted potential synergy with FLT3 inhibition; potency band ~1–100 nM in sensitive contexts

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to reduce JAK/STAT pathway signaling implicated in AML microenvironment contexts
additional_notes: Approved for myelofibrosis; variable single-agent AML activity; potency band ~0.1–10 µM

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases leading to increased histone acetylation and altered transcription promoting growth arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; potency band ~5–100 nM in many hematologic lines

mechanism: At higher concentrations exerts HDAC-inhibitory effects that can alter transcription and differentiation
additional_notes: Approved for epilepsy; noted that in vitro activity often requires high concentrations; potency band ~0.3–5 mM

mechanism: Activates retinoic acid receptors to promote differentiation and alter transcriptional programs
additional_notes: Abbreviation: ATRA; approved for APL; tested beyond APL; potency band ~0.1–10 µM depending on context

mechanism: Inhibits BCL-2 and BCL-XL to induce apoptosis
Inhibits the proteasome, disrupting protein degradation and inducing proteotoxic stress/apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma/MCL; potency band ~1–50 nM in many hematologic lines

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Approved for multiple myeloma; potency band ~1–100 nM

mechanism: Induces oxidative and proteotoxic stress and promotes apoptosis (classically in APL; broader AML interest)
additional_notes: Abbreviation: ATO; approved for APL; potency band ~0.2–5 µM depending on endpoint

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and activates AMPK-related metabolic stress pathways
additional_notes: Approved for type 2 diabetes; in vitro potency often high (~0.5–20 mM)

mechanism: Disrupts microtubule dynamics (tubulin-related effects) leading to anti-proliferative activity
additional_notes: Reported anti-leukemia activity in screens; solubility/formulation noted as important; potency band ~0.1–5 µM
